compiled_state.title: As an engineer, the agent checks prior-art framework at code-modification decision points
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Integrate the shared prior-art search fragment into the 3 consuming skills at their code-modification decision points.
**Why**: The framework only delivers value when agents actually consult it before writing code. Each consumer needs a reference at the right decision point.
**Scope**:
- IN: fix-bug SKILL.md Step 6, docs/workflows/prompts/review-fix-dispatch.md (resolution sub-agent), sprint SKILL.md Phase 5 sub-agent dispatch
- OUT: Creating the fragment itself (Story 1), eval verification (Story 3)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, fix-bug SKILL.md Step 6 references the shared prior-art fragment before code modifications
  ← Satisfies: "fix-bug references the shared prompt fragment at its decision point"
- When this story is complete, docs/workflows/prompts/review-fix-dispatch.md references the shared fragment for resolution sub-agents
  ← Satisfies: "code review resolution sub-agent references the shared prompt fragment"
- When this story is complete, sprint Phase 5 sub-agent dispatch template includes the prior-art fragment so sub-agents actually receive it
  ← Satisfies: "sprint implementation agent references the shared prompt fragment"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Fragment path references in 3 consuming skills break silently if shared/prompts/ is restructured
- [Scope] fix-bug SKILL.md already contains an Empirical Validation Directive (lines 39-52) with overlapping guidance. Integration must define the boundary — either the directive is superseded/absorbed by the fragment, or the fragment explicitly defers to the directive for its overlap areas
- [Scope] debug-everything reads fix-bug SKILL.md inline. Verify whether debug-everything's code-modification decision points are covered transitively through fix-bug, or whether a direct reference is also needed
- [Implementation] Sprint Phase 5 sub-agents receive instructions via task-execution.md prompt template. The prior-art fragment reference must be injected into the sub-agent dispatch prompt template so sub-agents actually receive it — a SKILL.md reference alone only affects the orchestrator

comments.body: Story complete: all tasks closed, prior-art fragment referenced in all 3 consumer decision points
comments.author: Test
